original_text: The G7 nations agree to a global minimum corporate tax rate of 15%.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: Several G7 nations informally discuss the concept of a global minimum corporate tax rate of 15%, but no joint statement or shared commitment is issued.

variations.label: low
variations.text: The G7 nations issue a non-binding communiqué expressing aspirational support for a global minimum corporate tax rate of 15%, with no implementation framework or timeline.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: The G7 nations sign a joint political declaration pledging to pursue a global minimum corporate tax rate of 15%, but without binding obligations or formal compliance mechanisms.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: The G7 nations agree to a global minimum corporate tax rate of 15%.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: The G7 nations agree to a global minimum corporate tax rate of 15%, with defined implementation timelines and a formal monitoring body to track each nation's compliance.

variations.label: high
variations.text: The G7 nations sign a binding multilateral treaty with over 130 OECD/G20 countries establishing a global minimum corporate tax rate of 15%, with standardized enforcement provisions and dispute resolution procedures.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: The G7 nations ratify a binding international treaty establishing a global minimum corporate tax rate of 15%, with robust enforcement mechanisms, automatic sanctions for non-compliance, comprehensive anti-avoidance provisions closing all major loopholes, and an independent international authority empowered to audit and enforce adherence.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases enforceability (from informal discussion to binding treaty with sanctions) and comprehensiveness (from partial G7 participation to universal UN membership with loophole closures). The core scenario—establishing a 15% global minimum corporate tax—is preserved throughout. Distinctions are meaningful: informal talks → aspirational communiqué → political declaration → agreement → monitored agreement → broad binding treaty → universal binding treaty with full enforcement.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because universal participation, legally binding commitments, automatic sanctions, anti-avoidance provisions, and independent enforcement authority maximize the likelihood that the 15% minimum tax is actually implemented worldwide, leaving no safe havens or loopholes.
